summaries.summary: The most-cited baseline method in this group is PaxosClassic, referenced across all three items (A1, A2, A3) as a Lamport-style crash-fault-tolerant SMR baseline evaluated on YCSB-A with three replicas, reaching 12k transactions per second.

The most-cited baseline method in this group is ZyzzyvaBFT, referenced across all three items (B1, B2, B3) as the comparison protocol evaluated on YCSB-A with three replicas under crash-only conditions, achieving 8k transactions per second.

The most-cited baseline method in this group is HotStuffMod, referenced across all three items (C1, C2, C3) as the chained BFT consensus baseline evaluated on YCSB-A with three replicas, reaching 15k transactions per second.

The most-cited baseline method in this group is PaxosClassic, used as the crash-fault-tolerant SMR baseline. It is evaluated on the YCSB-A workload at three replicas and reaches 12k transactions per second.

The most-cited baseline method in this group is ZyzzyvaBFT, evaluated under crash-only conditions on YCSB-A at three replicas. It reaches 8k transactions per second and is the protocol the authors measure themselves against.
The most-cited baseline method in this group is HotStuffMod, used as the chained BFT consensus baseline. It is evaluated on YCSB-A at three replicas and reaches 15k transactions per second.
